commands.observation: Rustdoc completed successfully with warnings denied and generated the openai_rust index under the isolated docs-build target directory.
Doc-tests passed; cargo reported 1 doctest from src/lib.rs succeeded with 0 failures.
All example targets compiled successfully under --all-features.
Example ran and printed env-based client configuration with the Responses quickstart model.
Example streamed a synthetic response transcript and printed created/delta/done/completed events plus terminal output.
Example ran and printed the configured JSON-schema structured output plus strict tool count.
Example ran and exposed status code, x-request-id, and content-type through the public response metadata surface.
Example ran and printed the embedding model, requested dimensions, and explicit float encoding.
Example ran and constructed the upload/vector-store flow parameters with current public types.
Example ran and printed compatibility chat, legacy completions, and preferred Responses models using the current public API.

The docs_contract target passed 3/3 tests, confirming guide/example files exist, commands in published markdown point at current examples, and fenced snippets validate against real markdown locations.
The package_contract target passed 2/2 tests, proving publish metadata is populated and local-only material is excluded by manifest rules.
Cargo metadata reported package name openai-rust version 0.1.0 with Apache-2.0 licensing, repository/docs/homepage URLs, keywords, and categories.
cargo package succeeded, packaging 183 files (1.3 MiB / 217.2 KiB compressed) and verifying the packaged crate builds cleanly.
The package file list included publish-facing assets such as README.md, LICENSE, docs/*.md, examples/*.rs, src/**, and tests/**, while omitting creds.txt, references/**, and .factory/**.

assertions.title: Public Rustdoc Builds Cleanly for Exported Entry Points

steps.expected: cargo doc --no-deps --all-features succeeds with no rustdoc warnings
steps.observed: Succeeded and generated the openai_rust docs index in the isolated target directory.

steps.expected: Exported documentation examples compile or run cleanly
steps.observed: cargo test --doc passed with 1 doctest from src/lib.rs and 0 failures.

assertions.title: Repository Examples Compile and Use Current API Shapes

steps.expected: Every file under examples/ compiles under the declared feature set
Core capability examples execute against the current public API shapes
steps.observed: responses_quickstart, responses_streaming, structured_outputs, request_metadata, embeddings, upload_to_vector_store, and chat_completions_migration each built and ran successfully.

steps.expected: Guides point at current example commands and files
steps.observed: docs_contract passed 3 tests covering guide/example existence, command references, and fenced snippet validation.

assertions.title: Cargo Metadata Is Publish-Ready and Apache-2.0 Correct

steps.observed: cargo metadata returned openai-rust 0.1.0 with Apache-2.0 licensing plus repository, documentation, homepage, keywords, and categories fields.

steps.expected: Manifest and root license file satisfy the publish-ready contract
steps.observed: package_contract passed both metadata and Apache-2.0 license checks.

assertions.title: cargo package Produces a Clean Publish Artifact

steps.expected: cargo package --allow-dirty succeeds and verifies the packaged crate
steps.observed: cargo package succeeded, packaged 183 files, and verified the crate build from the packaged source tree.

steps.expected: Publish-facing assets are present and secrets/local-only material are absent
steps.observed: cargo package --list showed README.md, LICENSE, docs/*.md, examples/*.rs, src/**, and tests/** while omitting creds.txt, references/**, and .factory/**.

assertions.title: Published Examples Match the Shipped Cross-Area API Surface

steps.expected: Published examples use only shipped API symbols and feature flags
steps.observed: cargo check --examples --all-features succeeded across the entire examples directory.

steps.expected: Env-based client construction, streaming, structured outputs, request metadata, upload flows, and compatibility migration examples execute against the actual public API
steps.observed: The responses_quickstart, responses_streaming, structured_outputs, request_metadata, upload_to_vector_store, and chat_completions_migration binaries all executed successfully and printed the expected public-surface outputs.

steps.expected: Published guides reference the same concrete example files and commands that compile/run
steps.observed: docs_contract passed, including checks for quickstart/maintenance/api-coverage references to the shipped examples.

summary: Tested 5 assigned publish-ready packaging/docs assertions through cargo-native validation. All 5 passed: rustdoc/doctests were clean, published examples compiled and representative examples ran successfully, manifest metadata was publish-ready and Apache-2.0 correct, and cargo package produced a clean artifact without creds.txt, references/**, or .factory/**.
